subject: Ecology
title: Temperate Deciduous Forests
description: A quick, practical guide to Temperate Deciduous Forests.

hook.text: Every autumn, temperate forests put on a color show—then shut down for winter.

buildup.text: Deciduous trees drop leaves to avoid freeze damage, entering dormancy until spring.

discovery.text: Fallen leaves decompose into rich humus, making temperate forest soil deep and fertile.

twist.text: Spring wildflowers bloom in a narrow window before canopy leaves block the sunlight again.

climax.text: This seasonal rhythm drives a boom-and-bust cycle of food, shelter, and migration.

punchline.text: Temperate forests live by the calendar.

quiz.question: Why do deciduous trees drop their leaves?
options: To avoid freeze damage in winter
